name: Baklava # 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the syrup by combining sugar and 3/4 cup water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, stirring to dissolve sugar completely.
2. Add remaining ingredients (honey, lemon slices, and orange slices) to the syrup, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes. Strain and cool.
3. Preheat oven to 325°F.
4. Remove phyllo leaves from the package and cover with a slightly damp dish towel.
5. Mix together walnuts, almonds, sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
6. Place two pastry leaves in an 15x10x1-inch jelly roll pan. Brush with melted butter.
7. Continue stacking leaves (14 total), brushing every other one with butter.
8. Sprinkle with 1/3 of the nut mixture.
9. Add six more leaves, brushing every other one with butter. Sprinkle with 1/3 of the nut mixture.
10. Layer six more leaves, brushing every other one with butter, and sprinkle with the remaining nut mixture.
11. Stack any remaining leaves on top, brushing every other leaf with butter.
12. Trim if necessary. With a sharp knife, cut into diamonds.
13. Starting at one corner, make 8 diagonal cuts at 1/2-inch intervals.
14. From the opposite corner, repeat cuts, cutting through only the top layers.
15. Bake for 60 minutes or until golden brown and puffy. Turn off the oven and leave the pastry in the oven for another hour.
16. Remove from the oven and pour syrup over the baklava. Following the diamond pattern, cut all the way through.
17. Cool completely in the pan to absorb the syrup. Makes about 36 pieces.
